sections.paragraph:
ἁλληλουϊά (also ἀλ-or-λούϊα for הַלְלוּ־יָהּ) Hb. loanword lit. praise Yahweh, transliterated hallelujah, liturg. formula of Israelite (Ps; Tob 13:18; 3 Macc 7:13) and then of Christian worship. Used as such Rv 19:1, 3, 6; w. ἀμήν (cp. the Hebr. of Ps 106:48; OdeSol 11:24; PGM 7, 271; a Christian amulet PBerol 6096 in Wilcken, APF 1, 1901, 430; ESchaefer in PIand I p. 29) vs. 4.—HEngberding, RAC I 293–99.—M-M. TW.

Hb. Hb. = Hebrew

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

PIand PIand = Papyri Iandanae—List 4

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 46
